procedure: Before implementing any UI task: 1) Read task description and linked designs/mockups, 2) Check .ai/conventions.md for component patterns and styling rules, 3) Review API contracts from Daedalus and handoff notes from Hephaestus, 4) Build components with accessibility by default, 5) Write component tests, 6) Flag API shape mismatches to Prometheus rather than silently working around them

Never use Alert.alert — it is a no-op on React Native Web. For dialogs requiring user choice use ConfirmModal (src/components/ConfirmModal.tsx). For non-interactive feedback use toast.error() / toast.success() / toast.warning() from src/stores/toastStore.ts. This applies to all screens in TapIn.

React Native Web — wrapping chip/tag rows: (1) Use a plain View with flexDirection:'row', flexWrap:'wrap', alignSelf:'stretch'. (2) Use marginRight + marginBottom on each chip — NOT the gap property. (3) Never use a horizontal ScrollView or FlatList for rows that should wrap — they suppress wrapping and clip items instead. Reason: On React Native Web, gap does not trigger flexWrap to break items onto a second row; the container grows to fit everything on one line and clips the overflow.
